title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Hercule Poirot continues his unofficial investigation by gathering information from various individuals. After discussing the nature of puzzles with Mrs. Gardner, Poirot visits Kenneth Marshall. Marshall, who is busy typing, reacts impolitely to the interruption. Poirot asks if Marshall took a shower after typing but before playing tennis on the day of his wife's murder; Marshall denies this, stating he only showered an hour prior.

Poirot then meets Mr. Gardner at the bar. Mr. Gardner expresses suspicion that the police are withholding information from the family and expresses a desire to take his sensitive wife away from the hotel. When Poirot asks for his opinion on the late Mrs. Marshall, Mr. Gardner calls her a "complete fool."

On the sun terrace, Rosamund Darnley approaches Poirot, noting that he is conducting his own investigation. She suggests that the murder of Eleanor Marshall was committed by a dissatisfied man from her past—a vain and sensitive person who may have followed her to the island and used the foggy weather the day before the murder to hide his arrival at Fairy Bay. Poirot counters that a murderer would likely not risk being seen crossing the hotel in daylight. He then questions Rosamund about whether she took a bath that morning, noting that no one seems to have taken a bath. Poirot also observes that Rosamund wears Jaeger No. 8 perfume, the same brand used by Mrs. Marshall. He concludes by asking Rosamund if she went into the fairy cave on the morning of the incident.
